policy_name: Maintenance Of Pregnant Desi/Indigenous Cow/Buffalo Ration Scheme For BPL Families Belonging To Scheduled Caste (SC) Category

sections.overview: This scheme by the Department of Animal Husbandry, Government of Himachal Pradesh provides a 50% subsidy on balanced ration for pregnant indigenous cows or buffaloes belonging to BPL Scheduled Caste families during the last trimester of pregnancy.
sections.objective: To increase milk production, improve health of pregnant cattle, reduce inter-calving period, ensure birth of healthy calves, and promote rearing of indigenous animals.
eligibility: Beneficiary must be a permanent resident of Himachal Pradesh
Must belong to BPL Scheduled Caste category
Must be a livestock owner rearing desi/indigenous or crossbred cows or buffaloes
Subsidy is limited to a maximum of two animals
benefits: 3 kg ration per day per animal
Support available for up to two animals
Ration provided for three months during last trimester
Increase in milk production by about 2 liters per day
Potential annual income increase of about ₹18,000
applicationprocess: Read scheme guidelines and accept terms and conditions
Upload required documents such as BPL proof and bank details
documents_required: Details of animals including ear tag numbers

faq.question: What is the objective of the scheme?
faq.answer: To increase milk production, improve cattle health, reduce inter-calving period, and promote indigenous breeds.

faq.question: Which department launched the scheme?
faq.answer: Department of Animal Husbandry, Government of Himachal Pradesh.

faq.question: Who is eligible under the scheme?
faq.answer: BPL Scheduled Caste livestock owners rearing indigenous or crossbred cows or buffaloes.

faq.question: What is the ration quantity provided?
For how many animals is the benefit provided?
faq.answer: Up to two animals per family.

faq.question: For how long is the ration provided?
faq.answer: For three months during the last trimester of pregnancy.

faq.question: How is the cost shared?
faq.answer: The cost is shared equally between government and beneficiary.

faq.question: What is the outcome of the scheme?
faq.answer: Milk production increases by about 2 liters per day after feeding the ration.

faq.question: How can one apply for the scheme?
faq.answer: Applications can be submitted online through the official portal.
